jobs.title: Survey Technician
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Ulteig is seeking a talented and motivated individual for our Survey Technician position with our 100% employee-owned company. In this role, you will partner with the Survey Crew Chief to support field data collection and project layout activities. You will also collaborate with the Professional Land Surveyor and office staff to process field data and develop land surveying and engineering deliverables.

This position offers strong opportunities for growth, especially for individuals interested in expanding their skill set and advancing toward a Professional Land Surveyor career path.

What You’ll Do

The Survey Technician supports the Survey Crew Chief in completing field surveys in accordance with land surveying standards and project-specific requirements.
Responsibilities include preparing equipment and supplies, coordinating with team members, and contributing to the successful execution of a wide variety of survey assignments.
In addition to field duties, the Survey Technician assists the Professional Land Surveyor and office staff in the development and delivery of high‑quality survey products.
This includes downloading and organizing field data, performing initial data checks for accuracy and completeness, assisting with base map creation, drafting survey exhibits, and supporting the preparation of Land Survey Plats, Legal Descriptions, Easement Exhibits, and other project deliverables.
The role also involves maintaining clear documentation, following established workflows, and collaborating closely with office personnel to ensure the timely and accurate completion of all survey-related tasks.
Ulteig provides hybrid office/remote work options to support flexibility and work‑life balance.

What We Expect From You

Associate of Science degree in Surveying or equivalent preferred, but not required
Land Surveyor in Train (LSIT) or Certified Survey Technician (CST) certification preferred, but not required
Entry-level candidates or individuals with up to 3 years of related experience are encouraged to apply
Participate in control, construction, boundary, topographic, route, retracement, and ALTA surveys across municipal, DOT, energy, and renewable projects
Experience operating survey field equipment (Trimble GPS, robotic total station, data collectors) preferred but not required
Experience operating remote sensing equipment (sUAS/drones, 3D laser scanning 3DLS) preferred but not required
Familiarity with AutoCAD, survey data processing software (Trimble Business Center), point cloud data processing, and Microsoft applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access) preferred but not required
Strong communication, time management, and organizational skills
Ability to perform prolonged walking, standing, bending, and stretching as required by field work
Ability to consistently maintain tools, equipment, and vehicles
Demonstrates openness to innovation by embracing and applying evolving technology and AI tools to enhance workflows, solve problems, and drive continuous improvement.
Must have authorization to work permanently in the U.S.

As you consider applying for a position at Ulteig, we encourage you to think outside the box – because we do! You might not meet 100% of the skills listed in a description, but we are committed to hiring people with exceptional talent, ability, and potential, and then creating an environment where they can become the best versions of themselves. We don’t want to miss out on the possibility of speaking with the next outstanding Ulteig team member, so please apply if you think this role is a great match for your unique skills and strengths. And, yes, relevant military experience is absolutely considered for transitioning service members.

What You Can Expect From Ulteig

Ulteig is a purpose-driven organization that has built a culture focused on people, both our clients and our employees, for over 80 years. Working at Ulteig is more than a job, opening opportunities to make an impact by creating and solving for a sustainable future. We recognize our success relies heavily on the dedication and focus of our workforce; this is why we make investing in our employees a top priority. We prioritize flexibility and staying connected to meet your needs and help you achieve your goals. We value your unique perspective, respect your individuality, and celebrate your contributions. At Ulteig, we take our success and the success of our clients personally.

We offer our team members:

Flexible Workplace
Employee Ownership
Competitive Pay
Comprehensive Benefits Package
Collaborative Environment
Innovative Culture

Our vision is to be the most trusted partners transforming our world’s critical infrastructure. Ulteig connects people and resources to develop compelling, integrated solutions across multiple Lifeline Sectors®, including Power, Renewables, Transportation, and Water. Ulteig is a trusted partner in engineering North America’s essential infrastructure and leverages its expertise with a wide range of public and private clients.

At Ulteig, we care deeply about our team, listening to their needs and ensuring they have the tools necessary to be productive, whether they choose to work remotely, hybrid or in the office. Regardless of where you work, you are empowered to build a workday that best suits your strengths. We strive to ensure a balance and separation between home and work life. We support a flexible working schedule so employees are able to focus on what's important to them.

If you would like to be a part of a company that empowers their employees, apply today!

Ulteig is a Drug Free Workplace

ACHIEVE | GROW | COLLABORATE

jobs.title: Research & Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Position Overview

The Research & Data Analyst will serve as a key source of market and business intelligence for CompoSecure's marketing and commercial teams. This role is responsible for transforming raw data into actionable insight - supporting strategic decision-making through rigorous market research, operational analytics, and financial modeling. From building ROI tools that win client confidence to tracking pipeline health and industry trends, this person will help ensure CompoSecure stays ahead of the market and operates with clarity and precision.

Key Responsibilities

ROI Modeling & Financial Tools

Design, build, and maintain ROI calculators and financial modeling tools that quantify the value of CompoSecure's products and programs for prospective and existing clients
Partner with sales and marketing teams to customize ROI models for specific client segments, use cases, and verticals - ensuring outputs are compelling, accurate, and easy to present
Continuously refine assumptions and inputs based on updated performance data, client feedback, and market benchmarks

Pipeline & Backlog Tracking

Own the reporting and analysis of sales pipeline and order backlog, providing regular updates to commercial and marketing leadership on volume, velocity, and mix
Identify patterns and anomalies in pipeline data that signal risk or opportunity, and surface findings with clear, actionable recommendations
Maintain data accuracy and consistency across pipeline reporting tools, ensuring alignment between CRM data and financial projections

Go-Get Tracking

Build and manage a go-get tracking framework that monitors progress against revenue and growth targets on a rolling basis
Develop clear visualizations and scorecards that allow sales and marketing teams to quickly assess gap-to-goal and prioritize pursuit activity
Provide regular cadence reporting to leadership with commentary on performance drivers, risks, and recommended actions

Market Analysis

Conduct in-depth market analyses including sizing, segmentation, share estimation, and growth opportunity assessments across CompoSecure's core and adjacent markets
Synthesize data from multiple sources - including third-party research, public filings, and internal datasets - to build a comprehensive and current view of the competitive landscape
Support strategic planning, new business development, and investor communications with well-structured, data-backed market narratives

Industry Trends & Research

Monitor and report on key trends shaping the financial card, payments, and fintech industries - including regulatory developments, technology shifts, and consumer behavior changes
Produce regular trend briefings and research summaries for marketing, product, and executive leadership to inform strategy and positioning
Establish and maintain a library of industry research, reports, and data sources that serve as a shared intelligence resource across the organization

Forecast Support

Provide analytical support for the revenue and demand forecasting process, contributing market context, historical trend analysis, and scenario modeling
Partner with finance and commercial teams during forecasting cycles to validate assumptions, stress-test projections, and present findings clearly

Salesforce (SFDC) Support

Serve as a data and reporting resource within Salesforce, building and maintaining custom reports, dashboards, and data views that support marketing and sales decision-making
Ensure the integrity and accuracy of key data fields within SFDC, partnering with sales operations to identify and resolve data quality issues
Support the integration of SFDC data with other analytics tools and sources to enable a unified view of marketing and commercial performance

Qualifications

3–6 years of experience in a data analytics, market research, or business intelligence role, ideally within financial services, fintech, or a B2B technology environment
Strong proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools such as Excel, Tableau, Power BI, or equivalent platforms
Demonstrated ability to build financial models, ROI frameworks, and scenario analyses from the ground up
Excellent research skills with experience synthesizing complex, multi-source data into clear executive-ready narratives
High attention to detail and a commitment to data accuracy and analytical rigor
Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting findings to cross-functional and leadership audiences
Self-starter with the ability to manage multiple workstreams and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Statistics, Marketing, or a related analytical field required; advanced degree a plus

About CompoSecure
CompoSecure, a GPGI business (NYSE: GPGI), is the leading manufacturer of Premium Metal Payment Cards and also offers best-in-class Authentication and Digital Asset solutions. The Company's offerings combine elegance, simplicity, and security to deliver exceptional experiences and peace of mind, enabling trust for millions of people around the globe. For more information, please visit www.CompoSecure.com and www.GetArculus.com.

We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your health, financial well-being, and work-life balance, including medical, prescription, dental, and vision coverage; FSA/HSA options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401(k) with company match; paid time off (PTO) and holidays; annual bonus opportunities; an employee assistance program (EAP); and opportunities for career growth and advancement.

The Ground Software Solutions Team develops and maintains custom ground software capabilities for our customers. We configure and extend Cockpit (Loft's Mission Control and Scheduling Software) for specific program needs, deliver custom features to support mission deployment and execution, and help ensure smooth operations by connecting product capabilities with mission-specific needs. We also drive product improvement by providing operational feedback and collaborating closely with Product teams.

This is an exciting opportunity to work on cutting edge technology, helping build modern automated infrastructure, bringing software and space together. As well as developing our future systems, the role will also be working on real-time issues on live missions, helping to solve problems as they occur. On top of this we also offer SatDevOps training to enable this person to actually operate our spacecraft!

Some Technical Information About The Position

The stack we are using: Python, Django, GraphQL, Kubernetes, Cue, C++

About the Role:

Own and maintain Ground Procedures, Configurations and Automation software tailored to specific programs and missions
Design and implement non-recurring features within Cockpit to support both Physical and Virtual Missions customers
Work closely with other Solutions teams and Software Product teams to ensure smooth integration and improve products
Help troubleshoot software integration issues found during development as well as during end to end testing, commissioning and operations
Participate actively in Satellite Operations and support Spacecraft Commissioning - Everyone is involved!

Must Haves:

Previous experience as Software Engineer, or Satellite Operations Automation roles involving software development
Ability to troubleshoot and effectively communicate issues and solutions
Experience with Python and Git
Docker

Nice to Haves:

Experience in Software Integration - Making all the pieces work together
Experience in or close to Satellite Operations
Experience with Satellite Scheduling concepts and or Flight Dynamics
GitLab CI/CD and Automated testing
Django

Some of Our Awesome Benefits:

100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance option for employees and dependents
Flexible Spending (FSA) and Health Savings (HSA) Accounts offered with an employer contribution to the HSA
100% employer paid Life, AD&D, Short-Term, and Long-Term Disability insurance
Flexible Time Off policy for vacation and sick leave, and 12 paid holidays
401(k) plan and equity options
Daily catered lunches and snacks in office
International exposure to our team in France
Fully paid parental leave; 14 weeks for birthing parent and 10 weeks for non-birthing parent
Carrot Fertility provides comprehensive, inclusive fertility healthcare and family-forming benefits with financial support
Off-sites and many social events and celebrations
Relocation assistance when applicable
Research shows that while men apply to jobs where they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other underrepresented people tend to only apply when they meet 100% of the qualifications. At Loft, we value respectful debate and people who aren’t afraid to challenge assumptions. We strongly encourage you to apply, even if you don’t check all the boxes

Who We Are

Loft: Space Made Simple.

Founded in 2017, Loft provides governments, companies, and research institutions with a fast, reliable, and simple way to deploy missions in orbit.

We integrate, launch, and operate spacecraft, offering end-to-end missions as a service across Earth observation, IoT connectivity, on-orbit AI, national security missions, and more. Leveraging our existing space infrastructure and an extensive inventory of satellite buses, Loft is reducing years-long integration and launch timelines to months. With more than 30 missions flown, Loft’s flight heritage and proven technologies enable customers to focus on their mission objectives.

With a growing fleet on track to reach 30 satellites by 2027, we are scaling up quickly across our offices in San Francisco, CA | Golden, CO | and Toulouse, France to meet accelerating demand for space infrastructure.

As an international company your resume will be reviewed by people across our offices so please attach a copy in English.

Most importantly, we are excited to meet you, and see if you are a great fit for our team. What we can’t quantify for you are the exciting challenges, supportive team, and amazing culture we enjoy.

Environmental Design Group is seeking a qualified Survey Technician who will work in the field to provide support to the crew chief and assist in the completion of the team’s daily tasks. Three or more years of experience is preferred but not required.
Must be proficient in MS Office, survey technology and equipment, and other industry-based software is required.

Operate and maintain survey instrumentation and data collection equipment; prepare and load assigned work vehicle for daily assignments
Use basic math skills to calculate distances, road elevations, and other computations; take detailed notes on site to support the team; place ground stakes at specific locations designated by Crew Chief to mark construction coordinates. Depending on soil and terrain conditions, this task requires significant physical strength and flexibility.
Prepare detailed drawings and maps using computer aided design (CAD) software to a high degree of accuracy
Research documents at relevant county courthouse to verify current property owner, adjacent owners, and boundaries established in old surveys, and easements; read engineering, topographic, and boundary plats, and other related plans, to gain understanding of the site to be surveyed or to provide information to other crew members; upload or download data files as needed for office use
Develop collaborative relationships with our clients and other industry professionals

Strong relationship-building and communication abilities.
Detail-oriented with excellent follow-through.
Professional, adaptable, and solutions-focused mindset.
Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities.
Ability to manage competing priorities independently and collaboratively.
Comfortable working directly with property owners, clients, and project teams.
Committed to quality work, integrity, and community impact.

Ability to sit, stand, and work at a computer for extended periods.

Must have a valid driver’s license and insurable driving record required in accordance with company insurance standards.

Collaborative, supportive team environment.
Client Experience program focused on trust and proactive communication.
Mission-driven projects that improve quality of life.
Professional growth opportunities, flexible work arrangements, and a culture where your voice matters.

Wellness programs and educational reimbursement.
Flexible in-office, hybrid, or remote work options (role dependent).
Paid time off, holidays, 401(k), and bonus opportunities.
Community engagement, volunteer initiatives, and career development programs.

Together, we’re making a difference—for people, communities, and the environment.

The Opportunity

Ulteig is seeking a talented and motivated individual for our Survey Technician position with our 100% employee-owned company. In this role, you will partner with the Survey Crew Chief to support field data collection and project layout activities. You will also collaborate with the Professional Land Surveyor and office staff to process field data and develop land surveying and engineering deliverables.

What You’ll Do

The Survey Technician supports the Survey Crew Chief in completing field surveys in accordance with land surveying standards and project-specific requirements.
Responsibilities include preparing equipment and supplies, coordinating with team members, and contributing to the successful execution of a wide variety of survey assignments.
In addition to field duties, the Survey Technician assists the Professional Land Surveyor and office staff in the development and delivery of high‑quality survey products.
This includes downloading and organizing field data, performing initial data checks for accuracy and completeness, assisting with base map creation, drafting survey exhibits, and supporting the preparation of Land Survey Plats, Legal Descriptions, Easement Exhibits, and other project deliverables.
The role also involves maintaining clear documentation, following established workflows, and collaborating closely with office personnel to ensure the timely and accurate completion of all survey-related tasks.
Ulteig provides hybrid office/remote work options to support flexibility and work‑life balance.

What We Expect From You

Associate of Science degree in Surveying or equivalent preferred, but not required
Land Surveyor in Train (LSIT) or Certified Survey Technician (CST) certification preferred, but not required
Entry-level candidates or individuals with up to 3 years of related experience are encouraged to apply
Participate in control, construction, boundary, topographic, route, retracement, and ALTA surveys across municipal, DOT, energy, and renewable projects
Experience operating survey field equipment (Trimble GPS, robotic total station, data collectors) preferred but not required

Strong communication, time management, and organizational skills

Demonstrates openness to innovation by embracing and applying evolving technology and AI tools to enhance workflows, solve problems, and drive continuous improvement.
Must have authorization to work permanently in the U.S.

Ulteig is a purpose-driven organization that has built a culture focused on people, both our clients and our employees, for over 80 years. Working at Ulteig is more than a job, opening opportunities to make an impact by creating and solving for a sustainable future. We recognize our success relies heavily on the dedication and focus of our workforce; this is why we make investing in our employees a top priority. Flexible Workplace

Our vision is to be the most trusted partners transforming our world’s critical infrastructure. Ulteig connects people and resources to develop compelling, integrated solutions across multiple Lifeline Sectors®, including Power, Renewables, Transportation, and Water. Ulteig is a trusted partner in engineering North America’s essential infrastructure and leverages its expertise with a wide range of public and private clients.

At Ulteig, we care deeply about our team, listening to their needs and ensuring they have the tools necessary to be productive, whether they choose to work remotely, hybrid or in the office. Regardless of where you work, you are empowered to build a workday that best suits your strengths. We strive to ensure a balance and separation between home and work life. If you would like to be a part of a company that empowers their employees, apply today!

ROI Modeling & Financial Tools

Loft Orbital is looking for a Software Engineer to join our Ground Software Solutions team.

The Ground Software Solutions Team develops and maintains custom ground software capabilities for our customers. Work closely with other Solutions teams and Software Product teams to ensure smooth integration and improve products

Participate actively in Satellite Operations and support Spacecraft Commissioning - Everyone is involved!

Previous experience as Software Engineer, or Satellite Operations Automation roles involving software development
Ability to troubleshoot and effectively communicate issues and solutions
Experience with Python and Git

Experience in or close to Satellite Operations

Falcon Program Coordinator - 1446

Job Link Copied

Full Time

On-Site

East Alton, IL, USA | Perryville, MO 63775, USA | Chattanooga, TN, USA | Grand Junction, CO, USA

Posted 9 days ago

Apply

West Star is the fastest growing maintenance repair organization in the industry and we recognize this is the result of our talented team of trusted employees working together to deliver customer service excellence. We are committed to providing our employees with personal and professional growth opportunities while fostering a culture of respect and well-being with a small company feel.

When you join our team we don’t think you should have to wait for your benefits to kick in. That’s why when you start, they start with you! This includes medical, dental, 401K match, time off accruals, weekly pay days and much more. We don’t want you to live to work, we want you to work and live.

What you can expect as a Program Coordinator at West Star:

The Program Coordinator supports program execution across multiple sites and operations by driving consistency, identifying improvement opportunities, and helping teams turn priorities into action. This role partners closely with Program and Project Managers, General Managers, DOMs, and support teams across the network to strengthen execution and alignment. The position plays an important role in standardization efforts across retail MRO sites and supports key initiatives tied to the implementation of the new Production Control system. This individual helps identify effective best practices across the enterprise and works with stakeholders to implement them across locations. They also support continuous improvement efforts by engaging operational and support teams, monitoring progress, and helping sustain change over time. The ideal candidate brings strong MRO operational experience, familiarity with continuous improvement and change management concepts, and the ability to use operational data to guide practical action.

This position will be an onsite role at any of the following locations: East Alton, IL; Grand Junction, CO; Chattanooga, TN; Perryville, MO

You Will Be ESSENTIAL To Many FUNCTIONS Including

Establish Standard Work (Baseline) Process:

Identify effective methods for safely and efficiently completing program inspections and checks using currently available resources.
Compare program performance across key operational KPIs using FP&A data and related analysis.
Define the baseline for expectations and future improvements, establishing the standard from which progress will be measured.

Continuous Improvement

Monitor program operations across sites and identify opportunities to improve execution, efficiency, and consistency.
Monitor and support the development of the tech-by-tech training program to encourage growth and capability development at all levels.
Promote consistent team management aligned with established baseline expectations.
Assist in initiating projects that reduce waste, lower operating costs, and improve overall efficiency.

Strategic Alignment

Engage program execution managers and leads across all locations to ensure teams remain aligned on baseline expectations and improvement initiatives.
Provide timely communication on progress, risks, and engagement to leadership.
Ensure program projects and initiatives support West Star Aviation’s broader vision and strategic plan.

Performance Monitoring

Monitor and track program metrics, KPIs, and progress tied to initiatives and improvement efforts.
Provide regular status reports on program health, execution progress, and benefits realization.

Daily Operations Oversight

Monitor individual site performance, from material procurement through final product delivery, to support customer demand and operational goals.

Risk Mitigation

Identify, understand, and communicate risks that could affect the program lifecycle or initiative progress, and assist in developing and tracking contingency plans.

KEY RESULTS
In support of the West Star 5-year strategy

Aligned production processes to support improved labor efficiency o Standard process for Production Control and digitization of maintenance task cards.
Execution of current, and implementation of future production control systems and processes
Improved program efficiency results across network locations o Established continuous improvement initiatives

Your Education

What you’ll need to bring with you

High school diploma required; bachelor’s degree preferred.

Certifications: A&P certificate preferred, or at minimum an Airframe certificate.

Your Experience

At least 5 years of experience as a lead technician, team lead, or in a similar role supporting specific program airframe models or related operations.

Experience working across functions and locations to support execution, alignment, and operational improvement is strongly valued.

Familiarity with office support and communication tools such as Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Power BI is required.

Your Initiative

We’re looking for someone who takes ownership, works with urgency, and performs well in a fast-moving environment where deadlines, shifting priorities, and time constraints are common.

Your Sense Of Responsibility

Attend work every day as scheduled.

Some travel between other retail and base maintenance sites is required

Notify supervisor in advance of shift start if unable to work.

Must have reliable transportation to get to work each day.

Follow all company and safety rules during performance of duties.

Other Particulars

Physical Requirements

Lifts 10 lbs. Routine walking, bending, and sitting.

Sit at a desk and/or computer for extended intervals.

Walk short distances routinely.

Routine or repetitive physical motion with arms and hands.

Basic personal computer operations.

Mental Requirements

Work with others in a professional manner.

Prioritize workload and work under pressure.

Coordinate projects.

Supervision

Will work under supervision of Director, Programs.

Partner with other business leaders (VPs/GMs), Program Managers, and Team Leads on multiple projects in a cooperative manner.

Partner with other key enterprise stakeholders (FP&A, IT, PMO, Facilities, HR, etc.) in bringing forward ideas and gaining support for initiatives.

Working Conditions

Office area or general areas of the facility and hangar.

Equipment Used Personal computer

Compensation & Benefit Information

The following information is disclosed according to state specific requirements. If the position applied to is not in a state with these requirements, the following information may not apply. The salary range for this position generally ranges between $125,000-$130,000. Actual rate will vary and may be above or below the range based on various factors including but not limited to location, experience and performance. The range listed is just one component of the total compensation package. Other forms of compensation include a semi-annual discretionary bonus, as well as medical, dental, vision, life, disability, 401(k), paid holidays and vacation time.

West Star Aviation’s Benefits

Benefits Effective Day One for Eligible Employees
Medical, Dental and Vision Coverage with Health Savings Account (HSA) and Flexible Spending Account (FSA) options
Life Insurance, Disability Benefits & Supplemental Benefits
401(k) Plan with Employee Contributions & Employer Match
Paid Company Holidays & Weekly Paid Time Off Accruals
Flexible Work Schedules include 5x8, 4x10 and 3x12
Weekly Competitive & Industry Leading Pay means you are paid more often
Bonus Opportunity based on Company and Site Performance
Internal Mobility & Career Advancement opportunities

This position will accept applications until 06/29/2026

Company Description

With over 78 years of industry experience, West Star Aviation stands as a leading independent Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul (MRO) provider. Employing over 3,000 professionals, we offer comprehensive services from our strategically located full-service facilities in East Alton, IL; Grand Junction, CO; Chattanooga, TN; Millville, NJ; Perryville, Missouri; and Statesville, NC as well as satellite locations in Denver, Houston, Minneapolis and Chicago. Our extensive capabilities encompass maintenance, paint, interior, and avionics services, supported by the largest Aircraft On Ground (AOG) network in the country, ensuring prompt and reliable mobile repair services nationwide.
